core_claim: Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431) is a Linux kernel LPE in the algif_aead crypto module that lets an unprivileged user write four controlled bytes into any readable file page cache. A 732-byte Python exploit edits a setuid binary and gains root. Portable, tiny, stealthy, cross-container. Same bug class as Dirty Pipe.

slides.subline: Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431). Four-byte page-cache write. Works on every Linux distro since 2017.
slides.visual_direction: cinematic, dark, single accent glow; text-free background; protected lower-third.
slides.visual_prompt: A single hard rim light catches a tiny Python script scroll in a dark void, the code glowing red as it rewrites a setuid binary icon below, premium dark editorial key art, the script held in the upper third with clean unmarked negative space filling the lower third.

slides.on_slide_copy: An unprivileged local user writes four controlled bytes into the page cache of any readable file. Uses that to edit a setuid binary and gain root.
slides.subline: Disclosed April 30 by Xint.io and Theori. CVSS 7.8. The exploit is a 732-byte Python script. Four steps to root.
slides.visual_direction: cinematic, dark, single accent glow; text-free background; protected lower-third.
slides.visual_prompt: Low raking light grazes a dark terminal in the dark, four lines of Python code highlighted as they execute, a lock icon below shattering, dark editorial photography, the terminal set high over a calm empty lower third.

slides.on_slide_copy: Portable, tiny, stealthy, cross-container. Four properties that almost never appear together. Works across all Linux versions and distributions since 2017.
slides.subline: Not remotely exploitable alone. But any local foothold becomes root. Page cache is shared across all processes and containers.
slides.visual_direction: cinematic, dark, single accent glow; text-free background; protected lower-third.
slides.visual_prompt: A focused side light illuminates four identical keys in the dark, each labeled PORTABLE, TINY, STEALTHY, CROSS-CONTAINER, all turning the same lock simultaneously, dark premium editorial, the keys centered in the upper area with generous negative space below.

slides.on_slide_copy: Open AF_ALG socket bound to authencesn(hmac(sha256),cbc(aes)). Construct shellcode payload. Trigger write to kernel cached copy of /usr/bin/su. Call execve(/usr/bin/su) to load injected shellcode as root.
slides.subline: Logic flaw in algif_aead introduced August 2017. In-place optimization lets page-cache page end up in kernel writable scatterlist for AEAD operation.
slides.visual_direction: cinematic, dark, single accent glow; text-free background; protected lower-third.
slides.visual_prompt: A hard top-down beam falls on four stepping stones in the dark, each glowing red as a foot lands: SOCKET, PAYLOAD, WRITE, EXECVE, dark editorial key art, the stones framed in the upper third over clean dark space.

slides.on_slide_copy: The exploit is reliable. No race condition. No kernel offsets needed. Same exploit works across distributions. Mitigation is blocklisting algif_aead module — but that breaks crypto functionality.
slides.subline: Dirty Frag and Fragnesia bypass this mitigation entirely by using xfrm and RxRPC paths. The bug class is wider than one module.
slides.visual_direction: cinematic, dark, single accent glow; text-free background; protected lower-third.
slides.visual_prompt: Cool light grazes a shield marked ALGIF_AEAD BLOCKED in the dark, but two red paths labeled XFRM and RXRPC route around it untouched, tense editorial composition, the shield held high with a clean lower third.

slides.on_slide_copy: Blocklist algif_aead module if not needed. But the real fix is patching the kernel. Every major distro released advisories: Ubuntu, Debian, RHEL, SUSE, Amazon, Arch, Gentoo, AlmaLinux, CloudLinux.
slides.subline: CVE-2026-31431 patched in mainline. The 2017 in-place optimization commit was the root cause. Dirty Pipe (2022) was same class, different subsystem.
slides.visual_direction: cinematic, dark, single accent glow; text-free background; protected lower-third.
slides.visual_prompt: A balanced split composition in the dark: left side vulnerable kernel with algif_aead slot open, right side same kernel with slot sealed and patched commit highlighted, a thin divider between them, premium editorial key art, the divider centered with generous negative space below.

slides.on_slide_copy: The 2017 optimization {{broke the page-cache isolation guarantee.}} [[Nine years later, a 732-byte script exploits it on every distro. The bug class persists.]]

caption:
Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431) lets an unprivileged user write four controlled bytes into the page cache of any readable file. A 732-byte Python script edits a setuid binary and gains root.

Disclosed April 30 by Xint.io and Theori. CVSS 7.8. The exploit is portable, tiny, stealthy, and cross-container — four properties that almost never appear together. Works on every Linux distribution shipped since 2017.

The flaw is in the algif_aead crypto module. An in-place optimization from August 2017 let a page-cache page end up in the kernel writable scatterlist for an AEAD operation over AF_ALG socket. Unprivileged process drives splice() into that socket and completes a targeted write into the page cache.

Four steps: open AF_ALG socket, construct payload, trigger write to cached /usr/bin/su, execve the poisoned su as root. No race condition. No kernel offsets. Same exploit across distros.

Mitigation: blocklist algif_aead module. But Dirty Frag and Fragnesia bypass this entirely using xfrm and RxRPC paths. The bug class is wider than one module.

Every major distro released advisories. CVE-2026-31431 patched in mainline. Dirty Pipe (2022) was the same class in a different subsystem.

The 2017 optimization broke the page-cache isolation guarantee. Nine years later, a 732-byte script exploits it on every distro.

Have you verified your kernel package includes the CVE-2026-31431 fix?

Follow for AI security breakdowns without the fake panic.

[Copy Fail, CVE-2026-31431, Linux kernel, local privilege escalation, page cache, algif_aead, Dirty Pipe, kernel hardening, exploit development]
comment_prompt: Have you verified your kernel package includes the CVE-2026-31431 fix?
alt_text: Slide 1 (cover): a tiny Python script scroll in a dark void, code glowing red as it rewrites a setuid binary icon below, empty lower third for headline.
Slide 2 (context): a dark terminal, four lines of Python code highlighted executing, a lock icon below shattering.
Slide 3 (risk): four identical keys labeled PORTABLE, TINY, STEALTHY, CROSS-CONTAINER turning the same lock.
Slide 4 (mechanism): four stepping stones in the dark glowing red sequentially: SOCKET, PAYLOAD, WRITE, EXECVE.
Slide 5 (gap): a shield marked ALGIF_AEAD BLOCKED with two red paths XFRM and RXRPC routing around it.
Slide 6 (defense): split composition, left vulnerable kernel with algif_aead slot open, right kernel with slot sealed and patched commit highlighted.
Slide 7 (takeaway): a 2017 commit hash with red crack spreading through years to 2026 where Python script waits.
Slide 8 (cta): a kernel version string on a clean dark end-card with open negative space.

sources.source: The Hacker News: New Linux 'Copy Fail' Vulnerability Enables Root Access
sources.supports: Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431) CVSS 7.8; 732-byte Python exploit; four steps to root; algif_aead flaw from August 2017 commit; portable across all distros since 2017; cross-container impact; no race condition; same class as Dirty Pipe; distro advisories; Bugcrowd David Brumley analysis.
Technical details on algif_aead in-place optimization; page-cache write primitive; four properties (portable, tiny, stealthy, cross-container); exploit reliability; distro coverage.

sources.source: The Hacker News: Linux Kernel Dirty Frag LPE Exploit
sources.supports: Dirty Frag bypasses Copy Fail mitigation (algif_aead blacklist); works regardless of algif_aead module; bug class wider than one subsystem.

narration.text: 732 bytes. Portable. Stealthy. Cross-container root. Copy Fail CVE-2026-31431.
Unprivileged user writes four bytes into any file page cache. Edits setuid binary. Gains root. Every distro since 2017.
Four properties never together: portable, tiny, stealthy, cross-container. Page cache shared across all processes and containers.
Four steps: AF_ALG socket, shellcode payload, write cached su, execve poisoned su. No race. No offsets. Same exploit everywhere.
Mitigation blocklists algif_aead. But Dirty Frag and Fragnesia bypass via xfrm and RxRPC. Bug class wider than one module.
Every major distro released advisories. Patched in mainline. Dirty Pipe 2022 was same class different subsystem.
2017 optimization broke page-cache isolation. Nine years later, 732-byte script exploits it on every distro.
Have you verified your kernel includes the CVE-2026-31431 fix? Tell me in the comments.

Four steps. Socket. Payload. Write. Execve. Root.
algif_aead blacklist bypassed by Dirty Frag and Fragnesia.

2017 optimization broke isolation. Bug class persists.
